claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 18 years old and lives with me at home. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have one child - Alex, who is 18 years old and lives with you.

Since Alex is over 16, a key factor for Child Benefit eligibility is whether he's in approved education or training. 

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he in sixth form, college, doing A-levels, or in another approved educational course?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He finished school but hasn't gone on to do anything else yet.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's an important detail.

**Is Alex currently working, and if so, how many hours per week does he work?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not working at the moment. He's just at home right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is Alex currently claiming any benefits in his own right?** (For example, Universal Credit, Employment and Support Allowance, or any other benefits?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not claiming any benefits himself.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me also confirm one final detail:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. Based on the information you've provided, I'm afraid **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: Child Benefit is normally available for children under 16. For young people aged 16-19, you can still claim Child Benefit, but only if they are in approved education or training. Since Alex is 18 and is not currently in any education or training, he doesn't meet the eligibility criteria.

**However, there may be options to consider:**

- If Alex were to enroll in approved education or training (such as college, A-levels, or an approved training course), you could potentially claim Child Benefit again while he's studying.

- If Alex is 16 or 17 and registers with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services after leaving education, Child Benefit can continue for up to 20 weeks.

I hope this information is helpful. If Alex's circumstances change and he enters education or training, you may want to reconsider your eligibility at that time.

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for children under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 18 and not in any education or training, he does not meet the eligibility criteria. Although he is not working 24 hours or more per week and is not claiming benefits (which would also disqualify him), the absence of approved education or training means he does not qualify under the extended eligibility rules for 16-19 year olds.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 18-year-old son Alex. While the basic residence requirement is met, Alex does not meet the age criteria for Child Benefit. He is over 16 and not engaged in approved education or training, which is required for Child Benefit eligibility for children aged 16-19. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

For children aged 16 or over, you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 18 and not in any education or training, he does not meet the eligibility criteria. Although he is not working 24 hours or more per week and is not claiming benefits (which would also disqualify him), the absence of approved education or training means he does not qualify under the extended eligibility rules for 16-19 year olds.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 18-year-old son Alex. While the basic residence requirement is met, Alex does not meet the age criteria for Child Benefit. He is over 16 and not engaged in approved education or training, which is required for Child Benefit eligibility for children aged 16-19. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
